content: A biological agent can be analogized to a MemoryBank for long-context conversation alignment: just as a biological agent persistently retains its pathogenic or functional traits to exert sustained effects on a host organism across extended interactions, a MemoryBank retains long-term user information and conversation history to exert sustained, aligned influence on an LLM's long-context responses. This frames autonomous agent action as rooted in persistent, structured memory that shapes all downstream interactions, rather than treating memory as a secondary add-on to agent capability.
